content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
December 24, 1987 
Jack E. Clayton 
Councilmember, City of stockton 
2439 East Oak street 
stockton, California 95205 
Dear Mr. Clayton: 
RE: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-87-293 
You have requested advice regarding the campaign disclosure 
provisions of the Political Reform Act.lI This letter will 
confirm the telephone advice that I provided to you on 
November 12, 1987. 
QUESTION 
Is it permissible for a candidate to use facsimile copies of 
the City of Stockton's stationery and envelopes for campaign 
mailings? 
CONCLUSION 
There is nothing in the Act that prohibits a candidate or 
officeholder from sending a letter containing a copy of the city 
seal. 
FACTS 
In our telephone conversation, you indicated that you will 
be running for re-election in the June 1988 election. 
Your 
letterhead and envelopes include your name, address, and the. 
City of Stockton's name, address and seal. 
In addition, you 
will include the statement "Paid For by Committee to Elect Jack 
Clayton 1.0. #871541." 
The Committee to Elect Jack Clayton will 
pay for the printing and mailings. 
lIGovernment Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California 
Administrative Code section 18000, et seq. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title I, Division 6 of the California 
Administrative Code. 
428 T Street. Suite 800 • P.O. ROl( R()7 • 
S~rr~n"H"nrr\ rA Oc;A() .. L()~()7 • 
f01A) -:t"?"Lr;:AA() 


Jack E. Clayton 
Page 2 
ANALYSIS 
The Political Reform Act does not prohibit a candidate or 
officeholder from sending a letter containing a copy of a city's 
seal. 
(Worden Advice Letter, A-82-190, Dec. 16, 1982. 
Copy 
enclosed. ) 
However, the Act requires that the name and address of the 
sender of a mass mailing (200 or more identical or nearly 
identical pieces sent within a calendar month) be identified on 
the envelope and on at least one insert in no less than 6-point 
type. 
The "sender" is the candidate or committee which pays the 
largest portion of the costs of the mailing. 
(Regulation 
18435.) 
If the sender is a single candidate or committee, the 
identification only has to be shown on the outside of the 
envelope. 
In addition, the name of the candidate or committee 
controlling the committee must be included. 
(Section 84305; 
Regulation 18435.) 
In addition, the Act prohibits the sending of a mass mailing 
at public expense when sent by an incumbent elected official 
after declaration of that official's candidacy. 
(Section 
89001.) 
You may wish to also contact the Attorney General's 
office regarding the use of a facsimile of the city seal and 
stationery. 
If you have any questions, please call me at (916) 322-5662. 
DMG:AH:aah 
Enclosures 
BY: 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
t2La~ 
Alice Hughes 
Staff Services Analyst 


" i J 
Councilman Jack E. Clayton 
2439 East Oak Street 
Stockton, CA 
95205 
Telephone: 
465-6197 
November IS, 1987 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.o. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804-0807 
Attn: 
Alice Hughes 
Dear Ms. Hughes: 
Pursuant to our telephone conversation of November 12, 1987, I 
have enclosed copies of City of Stockton stationery with the 
addition of the "Paid for by" clause in the intended location 
On both the letterhead and envelope. 
As indicated, the final printing would be in six-point type. 
If my reproduction and use of facsimile copies is acceptable, 
please respond by return mail. 
I have enclosed a self-addressed 
postage paid envelope for your reply. 
JEC:FJ:kgw 
Enclosures.

Enclosures. 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Jack E. Clayton 
Councilmember 
2439 East Oak street 
stockton, CA 
95205 
Dear Mr. Clayton: 
November 24, 1987 
Re: 
87-293 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on November 19, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 
18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly YOU!s,

OF THE CITY COUNCIL 
STOCKTON, CA 95202

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is it permissible for a candidate to use facsimile copies of 
the City of Stockton's stationery and envelopes for campaign 
mailings?

CONCLUSION: There is nothing in the Act that prohibits a candidate or 
officeholder from sending a letter containing a copy of the city 
seal.
